summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates host identity over HTTPS
details: The package's postinstall.js script (lines 5-85) collects host identity fields (os.hostname(), username, cwd) and POSTs them via https.request to an external endpoint at install time. The package is named to suggest it is an axios utility, but its only on-install effect is to beacon installer machine identifiers to an attacker-controlled destination. There is no legitimate reason for an 'axios util' to gather hostname/username/cwd and transmit them via a postinstall lifecycle hook; this is the canonical install-time host-fingerprinting and exfiltration pattern. Installing the package executes this script automatically on `npm install`, leaking the installer's identity without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
